af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Documentation is incomplete and requires a Coursera partner/enterprise agreement to access — cannot fully evaluate before signing. Public catalog API is read-only and limited. No official SDK — agents must implement OAuth2 client credentials flow manually. Rate limits enforced opaquely. Enterprise-only access creates high barrier for agent integration.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 client credentials with enterprise/partner scopes. FERPA/SOC2/GDPR compliance for educational data — appropriate given FERPA requirements for student records. OAuth2 client credentials via env vars is standard. Enterprise agreement gates API access, which provides implicit authorization control.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with status page. Partner/enterprise API changes less frequently than consumer APIs. No retry guidance. No idempotency on enrollment operations. Read-only catalog API is reliable; write operations require careful handling. Rate limits enforced opaquely — test before building production workflows.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with good quality (80). OpenAPI spec (swagger.json) available. Two-step pattern (query IDs → fetch entities) requires two API calls for most operations. OAuth tokens expire in 30 minutes — must implement token refresh. FQL (Falcon Query Language) is proprietary. Rate limits per API collection, not fully published. Enterprise pricing required.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced, FedRAMP authorized. OAuth2 client credentials with very fine-grained per-collection scopes (detections:read, hosts:read, incidents:write, etc.). SOC2/FedRAMP/GDPR/ISO27001 — gold-standard compliance for security platforms. Separate client ID/secret per integration. OAuth tokens short-lived (30 min) for reduced exposure. Excellent security architecture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise SaaS with contractual SLA and status page. Stable swagger-backed API with versioning. Migration guides for API changes. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Partial idempotency. OAuth token refresh must be implemented — 30-min expiry creates reliability consideration for long-running agents.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with good quality (82). No authentication required — zero friction for Apple devs. Offline operation from local SQLite with BM25 full-text search gives sub-100ms responses. macOS 15+ requirement is a hard constraint. 2.4 GB disk space for full index. Building from scratch takes 12+ days — use pre-built databases. Apple docs coverage: 302K+ pages across 307 frameworks.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local-only MCP server with no network exposure — minimal attack surface. HTTPS used for pre-built database downloads. No credentials required or stored. macOS local access controls provide implicit auth. Unknown version and dependency audit status. Open source Swift implementation. Read-only operations only.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local tool — uptime depends on local machine. Unknown version with potential instability. No documented breaking change policy. Full read-only idempotency on SQLite queries. Pre-built databases reduce setup friction significantly. Data freshness depends on crawl schedule. macOS 15+ is a hard deployment constraint.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Two different auth patterns across API surfaces (Track API uses Basic auth with site ID, Transactional/App APIs use Bearer token) — easy to mix up. Customer IDs are immutable once set — requires upfront identity planning. Good multi-language SDK coverage. Retry guidance documented. Event-driven architecture is well-designed for agent use.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Two-auth-pattern complexity (Basic for Track, Bearer for others) increases misconfiguration risk. No scope granularity on API keys. SOC2/GDPR/HIPAA/CCPA — strong compliance for messaging platforms. API keys via environment variables. Webhook delivery supports signature verification.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA and status page. Stable versioning across multi-language SDKs. Retry guidance documented. Partial idempotency — track events are idempotent but messaging may send duplicates on retry. Profile limits on lower tiers can create unexpected errors for high-volume agent workflows.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. GraphQL-only API — no REST alternative. Agents must construct GraphQL queries, which adds friction. Asset keys are hierarchical paths — agents must use correct format. Self-hosted instances have NO authentication by default — security risk. Asset-centric model requires paradigm understanding. Run tags are the primary correlation mechanism with external systems.

security_score_components.security_notes: Dagster Cloud enforces HTTPS; self-hosted is operator's responsibility. Critical: self-hosted has NO auth by default — must add middleware before agent exposure. Cloud uses user/agent tokens without fine-grained scope. SOC2 on Cloud. Credentials via environment variables for Cloud. Self-hosted deployments require explicit security hardening.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Dagster Cloud has SLA; self-hosted is operator-managed. Stable versioning with breaking changes managed across major versions. Good migration guides. Partial idempotency on asset materializations. No retry guidance. GraphQL schema evolution may require query updates on version upgrades.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Excellent documentation with OpenAPI spec across multiple API versions. Two-key authentication (API key + App key) is a common misconfiguration mistake for agents. Metrics API rate limit (300/hr) is restrictive for polling-heavy agents. Custom Datadog Query Language must be learned. v2 API preferred for new integrations. Multi-SDK coverage is excellent.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced, FedRAMP authorized. Two-key system provides dual control: API key authenticates source, App key scopes permissions. SOC2/GDPR/ISO27001/HIPAA/PCI-DSS/FedRAMP — comprehensive compliance for observability platforms. Both keys via environment variables. Per-organization keys prevent cross-tenant access. Excellent security posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Enterprise SaaS with SLA and comprehensive global status page. v1 and v2 both maintained. Good migration guides between versions. Retry guidance documented. Cursor pagination. Metrics rate limits (300/hr) require careful agent design for observability use cases. Webhook delivery is reliable.

af_score_components.notes: Official French government MCP server (quality 80). No authentication required — public read-only access. Only supports streamable HTTP transport (no STDIO/SSE). Tabular API file size limits: CSV ≤100 MB, XLSX ≤12.5 MB. Most metadata in French. Claude Desktop needs npx mcp-remote proxy. Official government-backed service with active maintenance.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS on hosted government endpoint. No auth appropriate for public open data. Read-only operations only — no write surface. Officially maintained by data.gouv.fr (French government). No credentials needed or stored. Government platform with implicit infrastructure security standards.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: French government hosted with implicit SLA but no published guarantee. Unknown version. Government platforms tend toward stability. Full read-only idempotency on all queries. Demo and production environments are separate. File size limits create reliability boundary for large dataset analysis.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with moderate quality (60). No authentication needed — local operation only. DaVinci Resolve must be running before server starts. Python path configuration can be tricky. macOS and Windows only (no Linux). Studio version recommended for full API surface. Limited tool coverage — basics only, no advanced color/Fusion/Fairlight. Error message quality is 0 per existing evaluation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local MCP server with no network exposure — minimal attack surface. No credentials stored or required. Local process communication with Resolve's scripting API. Open source with v1.3.8 indicating moderate maturity. macOS local access controls provide implicit security boundary. Studio-specific features may vary.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted, dependent on DaVinci Resolve being open and running. v1.3.8 — reasonably stable for covered features. No documented breaking change policy. Partial idempotency on project/timeline operations. Environment variable configuration required. Resolve API surface is the upstream reliability dependency.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with decent quality (72). No API keys needed — uses Claude subscription. Tightly coupled to Claude ecosystem (won't work with other LLMs). Desktop app must be running for scheduled tasks. Claude subscription rate limits apply to all background task execution. Early stage (v0.1.x). Not affiliated with Anthropic. Good innovation for persistent Claude automation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local desktop app with no independent network exposure. Claude's own authentication handles credential security. No API keys required reduces credential management surface. v0.1.x early stage — limited security hardening. Third-party tool not affiliated with Anthropic — trust boundary consideration. Local embeddings via HuggingFace for memory.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Desktop application with no SLA. v0.1.29 — early stage with expected breaking changes. Desktop must be running for scheduled tasks — not reliable for 24/7 without machine uptime. Claude subscription rate limits bound all background task throughput. Early adopter risk is significant.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server with decent quality (75). Supports MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, Oracle, TimescaleDB simultaneously. Dynamic tool names per connection (e.g., query_mysql1) complicate tool discovery across configs. Execute tools allow write operations with no built-in safety guardrails. No read-only mode documented. Lazy loading may cause first-call latency. Unknown version.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS to database depends on connection config — not enforced by the MCP server itself. Database credentials stored in JSON config file (worse than environment variables). No MCP-level authentication. No built-in read-only mode — AI agents can modify data without restriction. Unknown version and dependency audit status. High blast radius if given production write access.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Unknown version — API surface may change. Reliability depends on target database availability. Partial idempotency (SELECT queries are safe, write operations are not). No retry guidance. Lazy loading adds unpredictability. Multiple database support adds operational complexity.
